summary.intent: Address Codex review findings on PR #727 (checkbox adapter fixes for Leptos and Dioxus), resolve coverage failures, and get a clean Codex re-review pass.
summary.outcome: Commit 495a665d was pushed with the two Codex findings fixed and wasm regressions added; both review threads were replied to and resolved; Codecov patch coverage failure was identified as caused by new component crates being included, and codecov.yml was updated to exclude those crates.

repo: The repo uses a Codex review bot triggered via '@codex review' PR comments; it signals pickup with an 'eyes' reaction and can take 10-15+ minutes to complete or silently stall.

codecov.yml excludes examples, spec, xtask, and ars-e2e but did NOT originally exclude the new distribution crates ars-leptos-components and ars-dioxus-components, causing them to count against the 80% patch coverage threshold.
The repo's own GitHub Actions 'Coverage' job passed while Codecov's 'codecov/patch' check failed — these are separate signals with different thresholds.
Pre-commit hooks run formatting and lint checks and can take over a minute to complete silently.
Codex review threads are resolved via GitHub's GraphQL resolveReviewThread mutation.

code.finding: Added ignore rules for crates/ars-leptos-components and crates/ars-dioxus-components to exclude copied/distribution component crates from Codecov patch and project coverage accounting.

workflow: When a Codex review trigger stalls at 'eyes' for ~15 minutes, posting a fresh '@codex review' comment unsticks it rather than waiting indefinitely.
Polling GitHub APIs in a tight loop without sleep intervals wastes cycles and masks whether state is actually changing — 45-180s sleep intervals between polls are appropriate.
Downloading the CI LCOV artifact and diffing it against 'git diff origin/main...HEAD' gives precise per-file uncovered-line counts, which is more actionable than Codecov's aggregate report.
GitHub Actions API and REST API can return transient connection errors; retrying the same call without backoff usually succeeds on the second attempt.

friction: Pre-commit hook ran silently for over a minute with no output, making it impossible to distinguish 'running' from 'stuck'.
The first Codex review trigger stayed stuck on 'eyes' for ~15 minutes without completing or posting threads, requiring a manual re-trigger.
Multiple transient GitHub API connection errors during polling disrupted the review loop and required retries.
gh pr checks --watch=false stopped advancing job state mid-run, requiring a fallback to the Actions runs/jobs API directly.
Codecov's patch failure was not immediately obvious as a component-crate inclusion problem — required downloading LCOV, parsing the diff, and cross-referencing before the root cause was clear.
open_items: Codex second re-review trigger was still in 'eyes' at end of session — final review verdict (approve or new findings) not yet received.
CI jobs 'Release Verification' and 'Coverage' were still in progress at last poll — full green CI not yet confirmed.
The codecov.yml fix commit was created but the transcript ends before confirming the push succeeded and Codecov recalculated.
No review from human maintainers on PR #727 is mentioned — human approval still pending.
